# pw-router — Full LLM Context

> This file provides comprehensive context for AI assistants working with or integrating pw-router.
> For a concise overview, see llms.txt.

## Project Identity

- Name: pw-router
- Purpose: Minimal, auditable LLM routing gateway for regulated environments
- Language: Python 3.12, FastAPI
- License: MIT (open source)
- Repo: github.com/Protocol-Wealth/pw-router
- Author: Protocol Wealth LLC
- Version: 0.1.0
- Dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], httpx, pyyaml — pinned in requirements.lock

## Why It Exists

LiteLLM (the dominant LLM gateway) suffered a supply-chain attack in March 2025, has 50+ transitive dependencies, 800+ open issues, and a codebase impossible for compliance teams to audit. pw-router is the deliberate alternative: 4 dependencies, ~1,100 lines of core, readable by a compliance officer in an afternoon.

## File Structure

```
pw_router/
├── __init__.py      # Package init, exports __version__
├── __main__.py      # CLI: python -m pw_router / pw-router command
├── server.py        # FastAPI app, lifespan, routes, auth, security headers, body sanitization
├── router.py        # RouterEngine: model selection, CircuitBreaker, fallback chains
├── providers.py     # ProviderAdapter protocol + OpenAI, Anthropic, vLLM adapters
├── middleware.py     # MiddlewareContext, MiddlewareResult, plugin loader (namespace-restricted)
├── config.py        # YAML loader with ${ENV_VAR} expansion, config validation
├── health.py        # Background async health check loop
└── models.py        # Exception classes: ModelNotFoundError, ModelNotAllowedError, AllModelsUnavailableError

plugins/
├── __init__.py
├── example_redact.py   # Example PII redaction middleware
└── example_logger.py   # Example structured audit logger

tests/                  # 103 tests, all HTTP mocked via respx
├── conftest.py         # Shared fixtures, sample_config
├── test_server.py      # API endpoint, auth, sanitization, security header tests
├── test_router.py      # Model selection, circuit breaker, fallback tests
├── test_providers.py   # Provider adapter tests with mocked HTTP
├── test_middleware.py   # Plugin loading, hook execution, namespace restriction tests
├── test_health.py      # Health check loop tests
└── test_config.py      # Config loading, env var expansion, validation tests

requirements.lock       # Pinned dependency lockfile (all transitive deps)
```

## API Endpoints

### GET /
Service info, version, and available endpoints. No auth required.
```json
{
  "service": "pw-router",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Minimal, auditable LLM routing gateway",
  "docs": "https://github.com/Protocol-Wealth/pw-router",
  "endpoints": {
    "POST /v1/chat/completions": "OpenAI-compatible chat completions",
    "GET /v1/models": "List available models (requires auth)",
    "GET /health": "Router health + per-model circuit breaker status"
  }
}
```

### POST /v1/chat/completions
OpenAI-compatible chat completions. Drop-in replacement for any OpenAI SDK client.

Request body is sanitized before forwarding — only these fields are allowed:
model, messages, temperature, top_p, max_tokens, stream, stop, presence_penalty,
frequency_penalty, seed, response_format. All other fields (n, user, logit_bias, etc.)
are silently stripped.

Request (standard OpenAI format):
```json
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "system", "content": "You are helpful."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}
  ],
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "max_tokens": 2000,
  "stream": false
}
```

Response (standard OpenAI format):
```json
{
  "id": "chatcmpl-abc123",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1711000000,
  "model": "claude-sonnet",
  "choices": [{
    "index": 0,
    "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": "Hello!"},
    "finish_reason": "stop"
  }],
  "usage": {"prompt_tokens": 50, "completion_tokens": 10, "total_tokens": 60}
}
```

Streaming (stream: true) returns SSE in OpenAI chunk format with a 5-minute max duration:
```
data: {"id":"chatcmpl-abc123","object":"chat.completion.chunk","choices":[{"index":0,"delta":{"content":"Hello"},"finish_reason":null}]}

data: [DONE]
```

Auth: Bearer token in Authorization header. Returns 401 if missing/invalid, 403 if model not in client's allowlist.

### GET /v1/models
Returns models the authenticated client is allowed to use. Auth required.

### GET /health
No auth. Returns per-model circuit breaker status:
```json
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "models": {
    "claude-sonnet": {"status": "healthy", "circuit": "closed"},
    "local-llama": {"status": "unhealthy", "circuit": "open"}
  }
}
```

### GET /robots.txt
Returns `User-agent: *\nDisallow: /\n`. No auth.

### GET /.well-known/security.txt (and /security.txt)
RFC 9116 security contact (security@protocolwealthllc.com). No auth.

### GET /favicon.ico
Returns 204 No Content. Prevents browser 404 noise.

## Configuration (config.yaml)

Single YAML file. Environment variables expanded via ${VAR_NAME} syntax at load time.

```yaml
server:
  host: "0.0.0.0"
  port: 8100
  api_keys:
    - key: "${PW_ROUTER_API_KEY_1}"    # Env var expanded at load
      name: "my-app"                    # Identity for audit logging
      allowed_models: ["*"]             # Glob patterns: "*", "local-*", "claude-*"

models:
  claude-sonnet:
    provider: anthropic                 # openai | anthropic | vllm
    model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"   # Actual model ID sent to provider
    api_key: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}"
    base_url: "https://api.anthropic.com"  # Optional, has sensible defaults
    timeout_seconds: 120
    tags: ["external", "reasoning"]     # Used by tag-based routing rules

  local-llama:
    provider: vllm
    model: "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct"
    api_key: "${RUNPOD_API_KEY}"
    base_url: "https://api.runpod.ai/v2/${RUNPOD_ENDPOINT_ID}/openai/v1"
    timeout_seconds: 90
    tags: ["self-hosted", "client-safe"]

routing:
  default_model: "claude-sonnet"        # Used when request doesn't specify model
  fallback_chains:
    reasoning: ["claude-sonnet", "local-llama"]
    client-safe: ["local-llama"]        # Tag "client-data" routes here
  rules:
    - match:
        tag: "client-data"             # Middleware adds this tag when PII detected
      route_to_chain: "client-safe"    # Forces self-hosted models

health:
  check_interval_seconds: 30
  unhealthy_threshold: 3               # Consecutive failures → circuit OPEN
  healthy_threshold: 1                 # Successes in HALF_OPEN → circuit CLOSED
  check_timeout_seconds: 5

middleware:
  pre_request:                         # Hooks run before provider call
    - plugin: "plugins.example_redact" # Must start with "plugins." (enforced)
      config:
        endpoint: "${PW_REDACT_URL}"
  post_response: []                    # Hooks run after provider response
```

## Core Components

### Server (server.py)
FastAPI app with SecurityHeadersMiddleware (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options,
Referrer-Policy, Cache-Control: no-store on /v1/ routes). Request bodies are read as
raw bytes (enforcing 10MB limit on actual payload, not Content-Length header), parsed,
validated as JSON objects, and sanitized via field allowlist before processing.

### RouterEngine (router.py)
Model selection with circuit breakers and fallback chains.

select_model(requested_model, tags, allowed_models) → str:
1. Explicit model in request → use it (check allowlist)
2. Tag matches routing rule → select that chain
3. No match → default_model
4. If circuit OPEN → walk fallback chain
5. All exhausted → raise AllModelsUnavailableError (503)

### CircuitBreaker (router.py)
Per-model, in-memory. States: CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN.
- CLOSED: healthy, all requests pass. N failures → OPEN.
- OPEN: skip this model. After cooldown → HALF_OPEN.
- HALF_OPEN: allow one probe. Success → CLOSED. Failure → OPEN.
Resets on restart (safe default).

### Provider Adapters (providers.py)
Protocol: chat_completion(body, model_config, stream) and health_check(model_config).

- OpenAIAdapter: pass-through, changes base_url and auth header
- AnthropicAdapter: full format translation (system message extraction, stop_reason mapping, streaming SSE event conversion from Anthropic events to OpenAI chunks)
- VLLMAdapter: extends OpenAIAdapter (vLLM is OpenAI-compatible)

All adapters share a single httpx.AsyncClient with explicit pool limits
(max_connections=100, max_keepalive_connections=20).

### Middleware (middleware.py)
MiddlewareContext carries request/response data through the pipeline.
MiddlewareResult controls allow/block with error message and status code.

Plugin loading is namespace-restricted: only modules starting with "plugins." can be
imported (prevents arbitrary code execution via config). Plugin load failures are fatal
— the server will not start if a configured plugin fails to load (fail-closed).

Pre-request hooks can: modify request_body, add routing tags to ctx.tags, block with allow=False.
Post-response hooks can: inspect/modify response, log audit data, block.

### Auth (server.py)
API key from Bearer token compared with hmac.compare_digest (constant-time).
Each key has a name (audit identity) and allowed_models list (glob patterns).
Error messages are generic — no provider details leaked to clients.

## Security Model

### Input Validation
- Request body field allowlist: only model, messages, temperature, top_p, max_tokens,
  stream, stop, presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, seed, response_format forwarded
- Body size enforced on actual bytes (not Content-Length header, which is spoofable)
- Invalid JSON returns 400, non-object bodies rejected
- YAML config uses safe_load (no deserialization attacks)

### Auth & Access Control
- Constant-time API key comparison (hmac.compare_digest)
- Per-key model allowlists with glob patterns
- Generic error messages (no provider status codes or exception types leaked)

### Network & Transport
- Security response headers: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY,
  Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, Cache-Control: no-store on /v1/ endpoints
- Explicit httpx connection pool limits (100 max, 20 keepalive)
- 5-minute max stream duration timeout (asyncio.timeout)
- TLS termination handled by deployment platform (Fly.io)

### Supply Chain
- 4 runtime dependencies, all pinned in requirements.lock with exact versions
- Dockerfile runs as non-root user (appuser)
- No config.yaml baked into public Docker image

### Plugin Sandbox
- importlib.import_module restricted to plugins.* namespace
- Plugin load failures are fatal (server won't start without security plugins)

### Data Handling
- No request/response body logging by default
- No disk writes, no telemetry, no analytics
- No external calls except to configured model providers
- Provider API keys never logged

## Integration Guide

### As an OpenAI SDK Client
```python
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(base_url="http://localhost:8100/v1", api_key="your-key")
response = client.chat.completions.create(model="claude-sonnet", messages=[...])
```

### As a curl Client
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer your-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "claude-sonnet", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]}'
```

### Writing a Middleware Plugin
```python
# plugins/my_plugin.py  (MUST be in plugins/ directory)
from pw_router.middleware import MiddlewareContext, MiddlewareResult

async def pre_request(ctx: MiddlewareContext) -> MiddlewareResult:
    # ctx.request_body, ctx.client_name, ctx.tags, ctx.metadata, ctx.config
    return MiddlewareResult(allow=True)

async def post_response(ctx: MiddlewareContext) -> MiddlewareResult:
    # additionally: ctx.response_body, ctx.model_used, ctx.latency_ms, ctx.provider
    return MiddlewareResult(allow=True)
```

Register in config.yaml under middleware.pre_request or middleware.post_response.
Plugin module path must start with "plugins." — other paths are rejected at startup.

### Adding a Provider Adapter
1. Create class in providers.py implementing ProviderAdapter protocol
2. Register in create_adapter() factory
3. Add provider name to valid_providers in config.py
4. Write tests with respx mocks in tests/test_providers.py

## Development Commands

```bash
pip install -e ".[dev]"      # Install with dev deps
pytest -v                     # Run 103 tests
ruff check pw_router/ tests/  # Lint
ruff format pw_router/ tests/ # Format
pw-router --config config.yaml  # Run locally
```

## Deployment

Stateless — runs anywhere: Docker, Fly.io, Railway, bare metal.
Config from YAML file + env vars. No database. 512MB RAM sufficient.
Dockerfile runs as non-root user. Dependencies pinned via requirements.lock.
